message:
fix(fcp-e2e/llm_router): drive real handshake through connector + assert contract (br-ab8sb)

Closes flywheel_connectors-ab8sb.

Resolves the same class of defect as c9561961 (spotify), d18041eb
(terraform), and 9fbcbcb1 (seven more harnesses): the adapter called
handle_handshake(params) into the real connector but discarded the
response entirely, then synthesized a HandshakeResponse locally with
SessionId::new(), manifest_hash='sha256:llm-router-e2e', and
capabilities_granted copied straight from the request. Drift in the
connector's status, granted-capabilities surface, nonce echo, or
session_id wiring passed silently.

Fix matches the llm-router response shape: unlike spotify/segment
(ad-hoc JSON envelope with protocol_version/connector_id/capabilities
fields), llm-router serializes the full HandshakeResponse struct
back. So the adapter now deserializes the response via
serde_json::from_value::<HandshakeResponse> and returns it, rather
than reassembling from pieces.

Adapter-side assertions on the way through:
- serde_from_value failure → named FcpError::Internal with the
  deserialization error (distinguishes connector contract drift from
  connector-side errors in downstream logs)
- status MUST be 'accepted'
- nonce MUST round-trip unchanged
- no granted capability may lie outside capabilities_requested
  (the connector cannot widen the grant set)

Cargo.toml companion hunk: the test file references
fcp_sandbox::host_matches_allow_list at test body scope, but
fcp-sandbox was not in the dev-dependencies list. This blocked the
binary from compiling on baseline (error[E0433]) and thus from
verifying ab8sb's fix. Added fcp-sandbox to dev-dependencies with a
br-ab8sb-citing comment; pure bundling fix, no code change in
fcp-sandbox itself.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test llm_router_compliance_e2e —
3/3 pass (previously 0/? — binary did not compile).

Cc_1 / SunnyMoose IMPLEMENTER
